Package org.eventb.core.ast.tests

Class TestFormulaSpecialization

java.lang.Object
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.AbstractTests
org.eventb.core.ast.tests.TestFormulaSpecialization
public class TestFormulaSpecialization extends AbstractTests
Acceptance tests for specialization of formulas.

As most of the code is shared with rewriting, we do not check all cases, but rather check that specialization is actually implemented for all sub-classes of Formula, except assignments.

For each test, we specify a type environment, an input formula, a specialization and the expected result of applying this specialization to the input formula

    void
    dstIdentBlocksFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which has no substitution cannot conflict with the right-hand side of an existing identifier substitution.
    void
    dstPredBlocksFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which has no substitution cannot conflict with the right-hand side of an existing predicate variable substitution.
    void
    dstTypeBlocksFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing a given type which has no substitution cannot conflict with the right-hand side of an existing type substitution.
    void
    identBlocksDstIdent()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution using the same identifier in its replacement but with a different type.
    void
    identBlocksDstType()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution using the same identifier in its replacement but with a different type.
    void
    identBlocksFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which has no substitution cannot conflict with an existing substitution.
    void
    identBlocksIdent()
    Ensures that specializing a free identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same identifier.
    void
    identBlocksIdentDifferentType()
    Ensures that specializing a free identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on an identifier with the same name, but a different type.
    void
    identBlocksType()
    Ensures that specializing a free identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on a given type with the same name.
    void
    identDoesNotBlockFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which gets substituted cannot conflict with an existing substitution.
    void
    predBlocksPred()
    Ensures that specializing a predicate variable which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same predicate variable.
    void
    predDoesNotBlockFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which gets substituted cannot conflict with an existing substitution.
    void
    testAssignment()
    Ensures that assignment specialization is not supported.
    void
    testAssociativeExpression()
    Ensures that an associative expression gets specialized.
    void
    testAssociativePredicate()
    Ensures that an associative predicate gets specialized.
    void
    testAtomicExpression()
    Ensures that an atomic expression gets specialized (including generic operators).
    void
    testBinaryExpression()
    Ensures that a binary expression gets specialized.
    void
    testBinaryPredicate()
    Ensures that a binary predicate gets specialized.
    void
    testBindings()
    Ensures that bound identifiers are correctly managed when specializing under a quantifier with a quantified formula.
    void
    testBoolExpression()
    Ensures that a bool expression gets specialized.
    void
    testBoundIdentDecl()
    Ensures that a bound identifier declaration gets specialized (type only).
    void
    testBoundIdentifier()
    Ensures that a bound identifier gets specialized (type only).
    void
    testEmptySetExtension()
    Ensures that an empty set in extension gets specialized.
    void
    testExtendedExpression()
    Tests that an extended expression gets specialized.
    void
    testExtendedPredicate()
    Tests that an extended predicate gets specialized.
    void
    testFreeIdentifier()
    Ensures that a free identifier get specialized.
    void
    testIntegerLiteral()
    Ensures that an integer literal is not modified by specialization.
    void
    testLiteralPredicate()
    Ensures that a literal predicate is not modified by specialization.
    void
    testMultiplePredicate()
    Ensures that a multiple predicate gets specialized.
    void
    testPredicateVariable()
    Ensures that an predicate variables gets specialized.
    void
    testQuantifiedExpression()
    Ensures that quantified expressions get specialized.
    void
    testQuantifiedPredicate()
    Ensures that quantified predicates get specialized.
    void
    testQuantifiedPredicateNotFree()
    Ensures that a specialization containing non-locally bound identifiers properly renumbers de Bruijn indices.
    void
    testRelationalPredicate()
    Ensures that a relational predicate gets specialized.
    void
    testSetExtension()
    Ensures that a set in extension gets specialized.
    void
    testSimplePredicate()
    Ensures that a simple predicate gets specialized.
    void
    testUnaryExpression()
    Ensures that an unary expression gets specialized.
    void
    testUnaryPredicate()
    Ensures that an unary predicate gets specialized.
    void
    typeBlocksDstIdent()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing a given type which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution using the same identifier in its replacement but with a different type.
    void
    typeBlocksFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing a given type which has no substitution cannot conflict with an existing substitution.
    void
    typeDoesNotBlockFormulaSpecialization()
    Ensures that specializing a formula containing a given type which gets substituted does not conflict with a substituted identifier.

      Ensures that quantified predicates get specialized.

    • testQuantifiedPredicateNotFree

      public void testQuantifiedPredicateNotFree()
      Ensures that a specialization containing non-locally bound identifiers properly renumbers de Bruijn indices. For this, we check that we get the same result as a free identifier substitution, when using a specialization that does not substitute types.

    • testRelationalPredicate

      public void testRelationalPredicate()
      Ensures that a relational predicate gets specialized.

    • testSetExtension

      public void testSetExtension()
      Ensures that a set in extension gets specialized.

    • testBindings

      public void testBindings()
      Ensures that bound identifiers are correctly managed when specializing under a quantifier with a quantified formula.

    • identBlocksType

      public void identBlocksType()
      Ensures that specializing a free identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on a given type with the same name.

    • identBlocksIdent

      public void identBlocksIdent()
      Ensures that specializing a free identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same identifier.

    • identBlocksIdentDifferentType

      public void identBlocksIdentDifferentType()
      Ensures that specializing a free identifier which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on an identifier with the same name, but a different type.

      Ensures that specializing a predicate variable which has no substitution prevents adding later a substitution on the same predicate variable.

    • typeDoesNotBlockFormulaSpecialization

      Ensures that specializing a formula containing a given type which gets substituted does not conflict with a substituted identifier.

      Ensures that specializing a formula containing an identifier which gets substituted cannot conflict with an existing substitution.
